text:
From: Liam O'Neill <liam.oneill@shopfront.com>
To: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwood.ai>
Cc: support@shopfront.com
Date: 2026-12-05T09:12:00-08:00
Subject: Checkout latency and error spikes — urgent

Hi Ava,

We're seeing a significant degradation in checkout performance this morning during our peak window (08:45–09:15 PST). Key symptoms: 
- p95 steady but p99 jumped from ~450ms to ~2.8s starting ~08:50.
- error rate (5xx) rose to ~4.8% on checkout API for ~15 minutes.
- throughput (requests/sec) held roughly equal — not a traffic surge.

This is impacting conversions on the storefront. I've attached traces and a small set of sampled p99 payloads. Can you triage and advise whether this is on your side (model serving) or ours (request patterns)?

Attachments: [shopfront-checkout-traces.zip (1.2MB)], [p99-samples.txt]

Thanks — need a quick heads-up to share with our ops team.

— Liam

Hi Ben,

We're seeing a sharp increase in p95/p99 latency for the Redwood hosted endpoint across our peak batch windows starting ~08:50 PST. p95 jumped from ~210ms to 1.4s and p99 is hitting 3.6s. This started after a surge where new GPU instances were brought up. The customer-facing jobs are timing out intermittently (2% errors), and this is impacting reconciliation jobs.

Attached is a short trace bundle and the CloudFront logs (fintechco_trace_12052026.zip). Can you confirm whether this aligns with a scaling warm-up problem on your side? We need immediate mitigation and a playbook — our ops lead is standing by.

text:
From: Nora Patel <nora@novusai.com>
To: Ava Chen <ava@redwood.com>
Date: 2026-11-29T09:12:00-08:00
Subject: Sizing ask for hosted→dedicated migration

Hi Ava,

Thanks again for the product walkthrough last week. We just landed board approval to proceed with a Dedicated deployment for NovusAI's inference workloads and need to lock down sizing and a short validation/cutover window.

High level:
- Current hosted baseline: ~3.2M tokens/day, peak sustained qps ~1.8k for our chat endpoint (mostly 1–2k token responses).
- We expect seasonal spikes up to ~3.5k qps for two hour windows (Black Friday-ish).
- Required uptime SLA during business hours (PST): 99.95% to start.

Ask: Can you provide a concrete validation plan (traffic replay / soak), a recommended GPU fleet mix, and a contingency/rollback cadence for the actual cutover? We aim to do a 48–72h validation run in early Dec and cutover t...

text:
From: Samantha Price <samantha.price@novaware.com>
To: Ben Carter <ben_carter@redwood.example>
Cc: procurement@novaware.com
Date: 2026-11-30T09:12:00-08:00
Subject: Initial procurement constraints for upcoming Redwood deployment

Hi Ben,

Thanks for the demo earlier — our procurement team asked me to confirm a few constraints before we can move to a PO:

- Our standard net term is net60 for software vendors in EMEA APAC.
- We require a single master PO that references monthly invoices rather than per-invoice POs.
- We need the invoice cadence to be consolidated at month-end; line-item bundling is preferred.
- For discounts: we can only accept slab discounts tied to committed annual spend; prefer % tiers on annualized run-rate.

Can Redwood provide a template PO we can populate and confirm whether you can accept net60, and if there are any holdback or early-pay credits we should be awa...
